design_philosophy.core_loop: Answer trivia questions in reverse (clue-to-answer) format; select dollar values from categories; accumulate winnings across multiple rounds
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Knowledge recall under time pressure; risk-reward of wagering on Daily Doubles
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Compete as a contestant on the iconic Jeopardy! game show; test breadth of general knowledge
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Direct translation of the game show format to NES with minimal adaptation; fidelity to the inverse question-answer mechanic rather than traditional trivia presentation; competitive scoring emphasizes final Jeopardy wagering drama

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Question selection by difficulty (dollar values); wager amounts on Daily Doubles; opponent difficulty likely selectable

progression.unlock_mechanism: Category/clue selection within each game; multiple rounds per session following show format

world_building_techniques: Licensed game show branding and presentation
Familiar category structure from TV format

environmental_storytelling: Game show studio aesthetic (category board visual presentation)
Scoreboard display as social competition indicator

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Jeopardy! NES article)
